name: PyTorch
description: Dynamic computation graphs, Pythonic API, dominant in research and production. The current standard for most frontier AI work.

name: TensorFlow
description: End-to-end platform with excellent production deployment, TPU support, and large-scale serving tools.

name: JAX
description: High-performance numerical computing with composable transformations (JIT, vmap, grad). Rising favorite for research and scientific ML.  + **[Flax](https://github.com/google/flax)**

name: dm-haiku
description: JAX-based neural network library from Google DeepMind. Elegant functional API with state management, widely used in DeepMind's research. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Equinox
description: Elegant easy-to-use neural networks and scientific computing in JAX. Callable PyTrees with filtered transformations, seamless interoperability with the JAX ecosystem. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Diffrax
description: Numerical differential equation solvers in JAX. Autodifferentiable and GPU-capable ODE/SDE/CDE solvers for scientific machine learning and neural differential equations. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: vit-pytorch
description: Comprehensive Vision Transformer (ViT) implementations in PyTorch. Reference implementations of all major vision transformer variants including ViT, DeiT, Swin, and more. MIT licensed.

name: NumPyro
description: Probabilistic programming with NumPy powered by JAX for autograd and JIT compilation. Bayesian modeling and inference at scale.

name: Keras
description: High-level, beginner-friendly API that now runs on multiple backends (TensorFlow, JAX, PyTorch). Perfect for rapid experimentation.

name: tinygrad
description: Minimalist deep learning framework with tiny code footprint. The "you like PyTorch? you like micrograd? you love tinygrad!" philosophy - simple yet powerful.

name: PaddlePaddle
description: Industrial deep learning platform from Baidu serving 23+ million developers and 760,000+ companies. China's first independent R&D framework with advanced distributed training and deployment capabilities.

name: PyTorch Geometric
description: Library for deep learning on irregular input data such as graphs, point clouds, and manifolds. Part of the PyTorch ecosystem.

name: timm (PyTorch Image Models)
description: The largest collection of PyTorch image encoders and backbones. 900+ pretrained models including ResNet, EfficientNet, Vision Transformer, ConvNeXt, and more with training and inference scripts. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Triton
description: Language and compiler for writing highly efficient custom deep-learning primitives. Powers kernel optimizations in PyTorch, JAX, and other frameworks. MIT licensed.

name: GGML
description: Tensor library for machine learning. The foundational C/C++ library powering llama.cpp and many on-device inference engines. MIT licensed.

name: MLX
description: Array framework for machine learning on Apple silicon. Efficient unified memory design with NumPy-like API, automatic differentiation, and multi-device support. MIT licensed.

name: oneDNN
description: oneAPI Deep Neural Network Library. Cross-platform performance library of basic building blocks for deep learning, optimized for Intel CPUs, GPUs, and Arm architectures. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: ONNX
description: Open standard for machine learning interoperability. Open Neural Network Exchange provides an open ecosystem that empowers AI developers to choose the right tools as their project evolves. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: IREE
description: Retargetable MLIR-based machine learning compiler and runtime toolkit. Lowers ML models to unified IR that scales from datacenter to mobile and edge deployments. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Burn
description: Next-generation deep learning framework in Rust. Backend-agnostic with CPU, GPU, WebAssembly support.

name: Candle (Hugging Face)
description: Minimalist ML framework for Rust. PyTorch-like API with focus on performance and simplicity.

name: linfa
description: Comprehensive Rust ML toolkit with classical algorithms. scikit-learn equivalent for Rust with clustering, regression, and preprocessing.

name: Flux.jl
description: 100% pure-Julia ML stack with lightweight abstractions on top of native GPU and AD support. Elegant, hackable, and fully integrated with Julia's scientific computing ecosystem.

name: MLJ.jl
description: Comprehensive Julia machine learning framework providing a unified interface to 200+ models with meta-algorithms for selection, tuning, and evaluation. MIT licensed.

name: ModelingToolkit.jl
description: High-performance symbolic-numeric modeling framework for scientific machine learning. Automatically generates fast functions for model components like Jacobians and Hessians with automatic sparsification and parallelization. MIT licensed.

name: spaCy (Explosion AI)
description: Industrial-strength natural language processing with 75+ languages, transformer pipelines, and production-grade NER, parsing, and text classification.

name: Transformers (Hugging Face)
description: The de facto standard library for pretrained NLP models. 1M+ models, 250,000+ downloads/day. BERT, GPT, Llama, Qwen, and hundreds more.

name: sentence-transformers
description: Classic library for sentence and image embeddings.

name: tokenizers (Hugging Face)
description: Fast state-of-the-art tokenizers for training and inference.

name: fairseq2
description: FAIR Sequence Modeling Toolkit 2. Complete rewrite of fairseq with modern PyTorch APIs, native support for LLM training (70B+ models), vLLM integration, and first-party recipes for instruction finetuning and preference optimization. MIT licensed.

name: LibreTranslate
description: Self-hosted machine translation API powered by the Argos Translate engine. AGPL-3.0 licensed.

name: Pandas
description: The gold standard for data analysis and manipulation in Python.

name: Polars
description: Blazing-fast DataFrame library (Rust backend) - modern alternative to Pandas for large-scale workloads.

name: cuDF
description: GPU DataFrame library from RAPIDS. Accelerates Pandas workflows on NVIDIA GPUs with zero code changes using cuDF.pandas accelerator mode.

name: Modin
description: Parallel Pandas DataFrames. Scale Pandas workflows by changing a single line of code - distributes data and computation automatically.

name: Dask
description: Parallel computing for big data - scales Pandas/NumPy/scikit-learn to clusters.

name: DataFlow
description: LLM-ready data preparation system for turning raw PDFs, conversations, code, databases, and other sources into SFT, QA, and RAG-ready datasets.

name: NumPy
description: Fundamental array computing library that powers almost every AI stack.

name: SciPy
description: Scientific computing algorithms (optimization, linear algebra, statistics, signal processing).

name: CuPy
description: NumPy and SciPy-compatible array library for GPU-accelerated computing in Python.

name: NetworkX
description: Creation, manipulation, and study of complex networks. The foundational graph analysis library for Python data science.

name: cuGraph
description: GPU graph analytics library with NetworkX-compatible API. 10-100x faster than CPU for large-scale graph algorithms. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Vaex
description: Out-of-Core hybrid Apache Arrow/NumPy DataFrame for Python. Visualize and explore billion-row datasets at millions of rows per second. MIT licensed.

name: Datashader
description: High-performance large data visualization. Renders billions of points interactively without aggregation artifacts. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: Zarr
description: Chunked, compressed, N-dimensional array storage. Scalable tensor data format optimized for cloud and parallel computing. MIT licensed.

name: NVIDIA DALI
description: GPU-accelerated data loading and augmentation library with highly optimized building blocks for deep learning applications. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Narwhals
description: Lightweight compatibility layer between DataFrame libraries. Write Polars-like code that works seamlessly across Pandas, Polars, cuDF, Modin, and more. MIT licensed.

name: Ibis
description: Portable Python dataframe library with 20+ backends. Write Pandas-like code that runs locally with DuckDB or scales to production databases (BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL) by changing one line. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: skrub
description: Machine learning with dataframes for dirty categorical data. Preprocessing and feature engineering for heterogeneous data with seamless Pandas/Polars integration. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: Oxen
description: Lightning fast data version control for machine learning. Optimized for large datasets with efficient diffing, branching, and collaboration. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Pandera
description: Statistical data testing and validation for dataframes. Pydantic-like API for Pandas, Polars, and other dataframe libraries with type hints and lazy validation. MIT licensed.

name: Snorkel
description: System for quickly generating training data with weak supervision. Programmatically label, build, and manage training data using labeling functions and probabilistic consensus models. Powers Snorkel Flow and used by Google, Apple, and Intel. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: DuckDB
description: High-performance analytical in-process SQL database system. Fast, reliable, portable, and easy to use with rich SQL dialect support. Perfect for data processing and analytics workloads. MIT licensed.

name: FiftyOne
description: Visual AI development toolkit for visualizing, labeling, and evaluating visual datasets and models. Supercharges computer vision workflows with dataset exploration and model analysis. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Label Studio
description: Multi-type data labeling and annotation tool with standardized output format. Configurable interface for images, text, audio, video, and time series with ML-assisted labeling. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Delta Lake
description: Open-source storage framework enabling Lakehouse architecture with ACID transactions, scalable metadata handling, and unified batch/streaming processing. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Apache Iceberg
description: High-performance open table format for huge analytic tables. Brings SQL table reliability to big data with time travel, hidden partitioning, and schema evolution. Works with Spark, Trino, Flink, Presto, Hive and Impala. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Apache Hudi
description: Open data lakehouse platform for ingesting, indexing, storing, serving, transforming and managing data across cloud environments. Supports upserts, deletes and incremental processing on big data with built-in ingestion tools for Spark and Flink. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: lakeFS
description: Data version control for your data lake that transforms object storage into Git-like repositories. Enables atomic, versioned data lake operations with branching, committing, and merging for data pipelines. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Apache Airflow
description: Platform to programmatically author, schedule, and monitor workflows. Industry-standard orchestration for data pipelines and ML workflows with 500+ integrations. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Apache Spark
description: Unified analytics engine for large-scale data processing. In-memory cluster computing with high-level APIs in Python, Scala, Java, and R. Powers MLlib for distributed machine learning and Structured Streaming for real-time data. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Apache Flink
description: Stream processing framework with powerful batch and streaming capabilities. High-throughput, low-latency runtime with exactly-once processing guarantees. Ideal for real-time AI inference pipelines and event-driven ML applications. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Apache Beam
description: Unified programming model for batch and streaming data processing. Write pipelines once, run anywhere on Flink, Spark, or Google Cloud Dataflow. Portable, extensible, and enterprise-ready for AI data pipelines. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Scrapy
description: Fast, high-level web crawling and scraping framework for Python. Extract structured data from websites at scale with built-in support for handling common challenges like pagination, cookies, and concurrent requests. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: Temporal
description: Durable execution platform for reliable workflow orchestration. Build resilient data pipelines and ML workflows that survive failures and continue execution exactly where they left off. MIT licensed.

name: Luigi
description: Python module for building complex pipelines of batch jobs. Handles dependency resolution, workflow management, visualization, and Hadoop integration. Built at Spotify and battle-tested in production. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Mage.ai
description: Modern open-source data pipeline tool for integrating and transforming data. AI-native ETL/ELT platform with 100+ integrations, real-time monitoring, and collaborative features. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Hamilton
description: Declarative dataflow framework for building testable, modular, self-documenting data pipelines. Encode lineage and metadata directly in Python functions. Originally from Stitch Fix, now Apache incubating. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: D-Tale
description: Visualizer for Pandas data structures with a Flask back-end and React front-end. Interactive data exploration with charting, filtering, and code export. LGPL-2.1 licensed.

name: Sweetviz
description: Beautiful, high-density visualizations for exploratory data analysis in two lines of code. Self-contained HTML reports for dataset comparison and target analysis. MIT licensed.

name: TextAttack
description: Python framework for adversarial attacks, data augmentation, and model training in NLP. Augment datasets to increase model robustness and generate adversarial examples. MIT licensed.

name: uv
description: An extremely fast Python package and project manager, written in Rust. 10-100x faster than pip with built-in virtual environment management, dependency resolution, and lockfiles. Essential for modern AI/ML development workflows. Apache 2.0 and MIT dual-licensed.

name: Vector
description: A high-performance observability data pipeline for collecting, transforming, and routing logs and metrics. Real-time data processing with 50+ sources and sinks including Kafka, S3, and Elasticsearch. Ideal for AI/ML log processing and data ingestion. MPL 2.0 licensed.

name: scikit-learn
description: Industry-standard library for traditional machine learning (classification, regression, clustering, pipelines).

name: XGBoost
description: Scalable, high-performance gradient boosting library. Still dominates Kaggle and tabular competitions.

name: LightGBM
description: Microsoft's ultra-fast gradient boosting framework, optimized for speed and memory.

name: CatBoost
description: Gradient boosting that handles categorical features natively with great out-of-the-box performance.

name: sktime
description: Unified framework for machine learning with time series. scikit-learn compatible API for forecasting, classification, clustering, and anomaly detection.

name: StatsForecast
description: Lightning-fast statistical forecasting with ARIMA, ETS, CES, and Theta models. Optimized for high-performance time series workloads.

name: MLForecast
description: Scalable machine learning for time series forecasting. Train any sklearn-compatible model on millions of time series with efficient feature engineering. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: cuML
description: GPU-accelerated machine learning algorithms with scikit-learn compatible API. 10-50x faster than CPU implementations for large datasets. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: SynapseML
description: Distributed machine learning on Apache Spark. Scalable, composable APIs for text analytics, vision, anomaly detection with seamless Python/Scala/R/.NET integration. MIT licensed.

name: Darts
description: User-friendly forecasting and anomaly detection for time series. Unifies classical statistical models (ARIMA, ETS) with modern neural networks (N-BEATS, TFT, DeepAR) in a single scikit-learn compatible API. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: PyTorch Forecasting
description: Time series forecasting with PyTorch. Multiple neural architectures (N-BEATS, TFT, DeepAR) with in-built interpretation capabilities, built on PyTorch Lightning for distributed training. MIT licensed.

name: DataHub
description: The #1 open-source metadata platform for data and AI. Data discovery, governance, and observability with 80+ connectors, column-level lineage, and AI assistant integration. Originally built at LinkedIn. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: OpenMetadata
description: Unified metadata platform for data discovery, observability, and governance. Column-level lineage, semantic search, and team collaboration with 70+ data service connectors. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Amundsen
description: Data discovery and metadata engine from Lyft. PageRank-style search for data resources with usage-based ranking. LF AI & Data Foundation project. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: dbt-core
description: Transform data using software engineering best practices. The industry-standard framework for analytics engineering with 15M+ monthly downloads. Enables version control, testing, and documentation for SQL transformations. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: SQLMesh
description: Scalable and efficient data transformation framework with dbt compatibility. Features automatic data lineage, time travel, and virtual data environments for testing. Optimized for large-scale data warehouses. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: SLayer
description: Semantic layer for AI-powered data analytics. Allows AI agents to describe data models and query the data using an expressive format with measures, dimensions, and filters, without writing raw SQL. MCP, CLI, API, and Python clients. Embeddable as a Python library. MIT licensed.

name: Deequ
description: Library built on top of Apache Spark for defining "unit tests for data". Measures data quality in large datasets with constraint verification, anomaly detection, and incremental validation. Used at Amazon for production data quality. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Great Expectations
description: Always know what to expect from your data. Data validation, profiling, and documentation for data pipelines. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: ydata-profiling
description: One line of code for comprehensive data quality profiling and exploratory data analysis. Generates detailed reports for Pandas and Spark DataFrames including statistics, correlations, missing values, and data quality alerts. MIT licensed.

name: Soda Core
description: Data contracts engine for the modern data stack. Define data quality checks in YAML and automatically validate schema and data across your pipelines. Supports 20+ data sources including Snowflake, BigQuery, and PostgreSQL. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: TFX (TensorFlow Extended)
description: End-to-end platform for deploying production ML pipelines. Data validation, transformation, model training, and serving with TensorFlow. Powers Google's production ML infrastructure. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Doccano
description: Open-source text annotation tool for machine learning practitioners. Features text classification, sequence labeling, and sequence-to-sequence tasks for sentiment analysis, NER, and summarization. MIT licensed.

name: OpenRefine
description: Free, open-source power tool for working with messy data. Clean, transform, and extend data with web services. Formerly Google Refine. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: Optuna
description: Modern, define-by-run hyperparameter optimization with pruning and visualizations. Extremely popular in 2026.

name: AutoGluon
description: AWS AutoML toolkit for tabular, image, text, and multimodal data - state-of-the-art with almost zero code.

name: FLAML
description: Microsoft's fast & lightweight AutoML focused on efficiency and low compute.

name: Katib (Kubeflow)
description: Kubernetes-native AutoML for hyperparameter tuning, early stopping, and neural architecture search. Framework-agnostic with support for TensorFlow, PyTorch, XGBoost, and custom training operators. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Streamlit
description: The fastest way to build and share data apps. Transform Python scripts into beautiful web applications with minimal code. Widely used for ML model demos, data visualization, and internal tools.

name: Gradio
description: Build and share delightful machine learning apps, all in Python. The de facto standard for creating interactive ML demos with automatic UI generation from function signatures. Powers thousands of Hugging Face Spaces.

name: Marimo
description: A reactive notebook for Python — run reproducible experiments, query with SQL, execute as a script, deploy as an app, and version with git. Stored as pure Python. All in a modern, AI-native editor.

name: Hugging Face Accelerate
description: Simple API to make training scripts run on any hardware (multi-GPU, TPU, mixed precision) with minimal code changes.

name: DeepSpeed
description: Microsoft's deep learning optimization library for extreme-scale training (ZeRO, offloading, MoE).

name: FlashAttention
description: Fast exact attention kernels that reduce memory usage and accelerate transformer training and inference.

name: xFormers
description: Optimized transformer building blocks and attention operators for PyTorch.

name: PyTorch Lightning
description: High-level wrapper for PyTorch that removes boilerplate and adds best practices.

name: fastai
description: Deep learning library providing practitioners with high-level components for state-of-the-art results. Built on PyTorch with a focus on usability and transfer learning. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: PyTorch Ignite
description: High-level library for training and evaluating neural networks in PyTorch with an engine, events & handlers system for maximum flexibility. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: ONNX Runtime
description: High-performance inference and training for ONNX models across hardware.

name: einops
description: Flexible, powerful tensor operations for readable and reliable code. Supports PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow, NumPy, MLX.

name: safetensors
description: Simple, safe way to store and distribute tensors. Fast, secure alternative to pickle for model serialization.

name: torchmetrics
description: Machine learning metrics for distributed, scalable PyTorch applications. 80+ metrics with built-in distributed synchronization.

name: torchao
description: PyTorch native quantization and sparsity for training and inference. Drop-in optimizations for production deployment.

name: SHAP
description: Game theoretic approach to explain the output of any machine learning model. Industry standard for model interpretability.

name: skorch
description: scikit-learn compatible neural network library that wraps PyTorch. Seamlessly integrate PyTorch models with scikit-learn pipelines, grid search, and cross-validation.

name: Composer
description: Supercharge your model training. MosaicML's PyTorch training library with built-in algorithms for efficient training (FSDP, gradient compression, progressive resizing) and seamless distributed training on large-scale clusters. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: NVIDIA Apex
description: PyTorch extension for mixed precision training and distributed training optimizations. Powers many production deep learning workloads with tools for automatic mixed precision (AMP), distributed data parallel, and fused optimizers. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: RWKV
description: Attention-free language model architecture with linear-time inference, training code, inference examples, and an active open-source ecosystem.

name: Qwen
description: Canonical open model family from Alibaba with model cards, inference examples, fine-tuning guidance, and ecosystem links.

name: Qwen3-VL
description: Vision-language branch of the Qwen3 model stack with multimodal checkpoints, training recipes, and reference tooling.

name: MiniCPM
description: Compact open model family with practical code, deployment notes, and active edge/on-device focus.

name: Llama Models
description: Meta's canonical repository for Llama model documentation, examples, safety materials, and integration guidance.

name: GPT-OSS
description: OpenAI open-weight model repository with inference examples, recipes, and deployment guidance.

name: Mamba
description: State Space Model implementation with pretrained checkpoints, architecture code, and research tooling for efficient long-sequence modeling.

name: GPT-NeoX
description: Large-scale language model training codebase from EleutherAI with distributed training support and historical open-model importance.

name: GLM-5
description: Open-source mixture-of-experts language model family optimized for long-horizon planning, agentic tasks, and coding. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: openai/CLIP
description: Canonical OpenAI contrastive vision-language model codebase with pretrained checkpoints and practical reference implementation for image-text retrieval and classification.

name: OpenCLIP
description: Open implementation of CLIP with training code, pretrained models, and zero-shot evaluation tooling.

name: OmniParser
description: Vision-based GUI parsing model and tooling for computer-use agents.

name: MiniCPM-V
description: Compact vision-language model family with edge-focused deployment examples and strong OCR-oriented use cases.

name: Eagle
description: NVIDIA multimodal model codebase with open checkpoints and reusable research materials for vision-language and video-language tasks.

name: Moondream
description: Small vision-language model with practical inference examples for edge and real-time image understanding.

name: VILA
description: NVIDIA vision-language model family with training, evaluation, and deployment materials across edge and datacenter settings.

name: Depth Anything V2
description: Monocular depth-estimation foundation model with practical inference code and broad computer-vision reuse.

name: NVIDIA Cosmos
description: Open platform of world models, tokenizers, and post-training tools designed for physical AI, robotics, and autonomous systems.

name: Whisper
description: Canonical open speech-to-text model codebase with widespread ecosystem support and many downstream implementations.

name: FunASR
description: Speech recognition toolkit with pretrained models, streaming support, diarization, VAD, and production-oriented examples.

name: NVIDIA NeMo
description: Scalable framework and model codebase for speech, language, and multimodal AI with recipes and deployment guidance.

name: Sherpa-ONNX
description: Complete speech toolkit with ASR, TTS, diarization, source separation, and VAD across embedded and edge environments via ONNX Runtime.

name: MOSS-TTS
description: Open speech and sound generation family focused on expressive, long-form text-to-speech with streaming and multi-speaker support.

name: VoxCPM
description: Open-sourced tokenizer-free multilingual speech synthesis model with high-quality TTS and style transfer workflows.

name: VibeVoice
description: Open Frontier Voice AI toolkit spanning speech understanding, generation, and multilingual TTS workflows, with active research and deployment tooling.

name: SpeechBrain
description: PyTorch speech toolkit with recipes for ASR, TTS, speaker recognition, and speech enhancement.

name: llama.cpp
description: Pure C/C++ inference engine with GGUF format support. The gold standard for CPU/GPU/Apple Silicon on-device running. Includes llama-server for OpenAI-compatible API. Now at 100K+ stars.

name: Ollama
description: Dead-simple local LLM runner with a one-line install, model registry, and OpenAI-compatible API.

name: Foundry Local
description: Open-source on-device AI platform covering discovery, model running, sandboxed execution, and evaluation of open models.

name: Potato OS
description: Linux distribution for fully local AI inference on Raspberry Pi 5 and 4, optimized for running open models at the edge.

name: MLC-LLM
description: Deployment engine that compiles and runs LLMs across browsers, mobile devices, and local hardware.

name: WebLLM
description: High-performance in-browser LLM inference engine. Runs models directly in the browser with WebGPU acceleration.

name: llama-cpp-python
description: Official Python bindings for llama.cpp.

name: KoboldCpp
description: User-friendly llama.cpp fork focused on role-playing and creative writing.

name: RamaLama
description: Container-centric tool for simplifying local AI model serving. Automatically detects GPUs, pulls optimized container images, and runs models securely in rootless containers with enterprise-grade isolation.

name: LiteRT
description: Google's production-ready on-device ML and GenAI deployment framework. Supports Android, iOS, Web, Desktop, and IoT targets with GPU/NPU acceleration via a unified edge-first runtime. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: LiteRT-LM
description: Production-ready runtime for deploying LLMs on edge devices with low-latency inference and optimized hardware paths for mobile and embedded platforms.

name: exo
description: Run frontier AI locally by connecting all your devices into an AI cluster. Features automatic device discovery, RDMA over Thunderbolt for 99% latency reduction, topology-aware auto parallel, and tensor parallelism. Uses MLX backend for distributed inference across Apple Silicon devices. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: omlx
description: Apple-centric inference server for local-first AI workflows with model management, GPU orchestration, and OpenAI-compatible APIs for self-hosted deployment. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: llm-d
description: Kubernetes-native distributed LLM inference framework. Donated to CNCF by RedHat, Google, and IBM. Intelligent scheduling, KV-cache optimization, and state-of-the-art performance across accelerators.

name: LMDeploy
description: Toolkit for compressing, deploying, and serving LLMs from OpenMMLab. 4-bit inference with 2.4x higher performance than FP16, distributed multi-model serving across machines.

name: vLLM
description: State-of-the-art serving engine with PagedAttention and continuous batching. Currently the fastest production-grade LLM server.

name: vLLM-Omni
description: Multi-modal inference stack extending vLLM for image, audio, and video generation workloads with a unified serving interface. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: LMCache
description: Supercharge LLM inference with the fastest KV Cache layer. 3-10x delay savings and GPU cycle reduction for multi-round QA and RAG. Integrates seamlessly with vLLM for distributed, high-throughput deployments. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: vLLM Production Stack
description: Kubernetes-native production stack for vLLM inference. Automated deployment, autoscaling, and monitoring for enterprise-grade LLM serving. Built by the vLLM team for seamless integration.

name: Open Model Engine (OME)
description: Kubernetes operator for LLM serving with GPU scheduling and model lifecycle management across vLLM, SGLang, and TensorRT-LLM.

name: nano-vLLM
description: Minimalist vLLM implementation in ~1,200 lines of Python. Educational yet performant with prefix caching, tensor parallelism, and CUDA graph acceleration. Comparable inference speeds to full vLLM. MIT licensed.

name: SGLang
description: Next-gen serving framework with RadixAttention. Powers xAI's production workloads at 100K+ GPUs scale.

name: TensorRT-LLM
description: NVIDIA's official high-performance inference backend.

name: Aphrodite Engine
description: vLLM fork optimized for role-play and creative writing. Supports extensive quantization methods (AQLM, AWQ, GPTQ, GGUF, FP8) and modern samplers. Active development with multi-LoRA and speculative decoding support.

name: AIBrix
description: Cost-efficient and pluggable infrastructure components for GenAI inference. Kubernetes-native control plane for vLLM with distributed KV cache, heterogeneous GPU serving, and intelligent routing. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Triton Inference Server
description: NVIDIA's production-grade open-source inference serving software. Supports multiple frameworks (TensorRT, PyTorch, ONNX) with optimized cloud and edge deployment.

name: mistral.rs
description: Fast, flexible Rust-native LLM inference engine built on Candle. Supports text, vision, audio, image generation, and embeddings with hardware-aware auto-tuning.

name: KTransformers
description: Flexible framework for heterogeneous CPU-GPU LLM inference and fine-tuning. Enables running large MoE models by offloading experts to CPU with BF16/FP8 precision support.

name: llamafile
description: Mozilla's single-file distributable LLM solution. Bundle model weights, inference engine, and runtime into one portable executable that runs on six OSes without installation.

name: Xinference
description: Unified, production-ready inference API for LLMs, speech, and multimodal models. Drop-in GPT replacement with single-line code changes. Supports thousands of models with auto-batching and distributed inference.

name: RTP-LLM (Alibaba)
description: Alibaba's high-performance LLM inference acceleration engine. Powers production LLM services across Taobao, Tmall, and Alibaba's international AI platform. Supports PagedAttention, FlashAttention, FlashDecoding, INT8/INT4 quantization, and heterogeneous hardware (GPU/ARM CPU/Intel). Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: LitServe (Lightning AI)
description: Minimal Python framework for building custom AI inference servers with full control over logic, batching, and scaling. 2x faster than FastAPI with built-in batching, streaming, and multi-GPU autoscaling. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: LightLLM
description: Pure Python-based LLM inference and serving framework with lightweight design, easy extensibility, and high-speed performance. Integrates optimizations from FasterTransformer, TGI, vLLM, and SGLang.

name: TabbyAPI
description: FastAPI-based API server for ExLlamaV2/V3 backends. OpenAI-compatible API with support for model loading/unloading, embeddings, speculative decoding, multi-LoRA, and streaming.

name: GPUStack
description: GPU cluster manager that orchestrates inference engines like vLLM and SGLang. Automated engine selection, parameter optimization, and distributed multi-GPU deployment for high-performance AI workloads.

name: One-API

name: no-mistakes
description: A local Git proxy and validation pipeline that runs AI-driven checks and applies fixes in a temporary worktree before forwarding pushes and opening clean PRs. MIT licensed.

name: Helicone
description: Open-source LLM observability platform with prompt management, versioning, and experimentation. One-line integration, YC W23 company. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: GEPA
description: Reflective prompt evolution optimizer using natural language reflection and Pareto frontier learning. Outperforms reinforcement learning for prompt optimization. Integrated with DSPY and MLflow. MIT licensed.

name: Papers with Code
description: Definitive database linking papers to open code and datasets.

name: Hugging Face Papers
description: Daily-updated feed of the latest arXiv papers with open weights.

name: Open LLM Leaderboard (Hugging Face)
description: Real-time ranking of open models.

name: Hugging Face Discussions
description: Largest open AI forum.

name: AI Engineering from Scratch (rohitg00)

name: Prompt Engineering Guide (DAIR-AI)
description: Comprehensive guides, papers, lessons, and notebooks for prompt engineering, context engineering, RAG, and AI Agents. The definitive open-source resource for learning prompt engineering with 3M+ learners. MIT licensed.

name: Start Machine Learning (louisfb01)
description: A complete guide to start and improve in machine learning and AI in 2026 without any background. Curated learning path with the latest news, state-of-the-art techniques, and comprehensive resources for beginners. MIT licensed.

description: Comprehensive learning path and template guide for Claude Code, covering setup, hooks, custom skills, and MCP server integrations. MIT licensed.

name: LangChain Academy
description: Free courses on agents and RAG.

name: Data Science for Beginners (Microsoft)
description: 10-week, 20-lesson curriculum on data science fundamentals. Covers data preparation, visualization, modeling, and deployment with practical projects.

name: Learn PyTorch for Deep Learning (Zero to Mastery)

name: NLP Course (Yandex Data School)

description: Practical hands-on course about Large Language Models and their applications. Covers Chatbots, Code Generation, OpenAI API, Hugging Face, Vector databases, LangChain, Fine Tuning, PEFT, LoRA, QLoRA. MIT licensed.

name: TensorFlow Tutorials
description: Official guides for beginners to advanced users.

name: Hugging Face Transformers Notebooks
description: Run Transformers, Datasets, and more in Colab.

description: The definitive curated list of machine learning frameworks, libraries and software organized by language. Covers Python, C++, Java, JavaScript, and more with comprehensive coverage of the ML ecosystem. CC0-1.0 licensed.

name: Awesome Artificial Intelligence

name: Andrej Karpathy Skills
